name: Haiti (civil flag)

colors.symbolism: Haitians of African descent and freedom
Haitians of mixed descent and the blood of the revolution

description: Two horizontal stripes of equal width — blue over red — without the coat of arms in the centre.
history: The civil flag of Haiti is the bicolour blue and red horizontal flag without the central coat of arms. It is the version flown by private citizens, while the state flag (the most familiar form) bears the trophy-and-palm coat of arms in the centre.

features.symbolism: blue and red taken from the French tricolour with the white removed at the Congress of Arcahaie (1803), symbolising the union of black and mixed-race Haitians against French colonial rule
